skill.name: Azure Key Vault Secrets SDK for Rust
skill.description: A Rust SDK for Azure Key Vault secrets management. Provides functionality to list, get, set, delete, and manage secrets in Azure Key Vault with async support.
skill.summary: Rust SDK for Azure Key Vault secrets management with async operations
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill containing only documentation (SKILL.md). No executable code present. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 security issues. The skill describes a Rust SDK for Azure Key Vault and poses no security risk.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Key Vault Secrets in Rust
content.value_statement: This skill provides AI-assisted guidance for using the Azure Key Vault Secrets SDK in Rust. It helps developers integrate secure secret management into their Rust applications without manually searching through documentation.
actual_capabilities: Generate code examples for Azure Key Vault secret operations in Rust
Explain how to authenticate with Azure Key Vault using different methods
Provide guidance on listing, getting, setting, and deleting secrets
Help troubleshoot common integration issues with the Azure SDK
Explain async patterns for secret management in Rust applications
Describe best practices for secret rotation and lifecycle management
limitations: Does not execute actual Azure API calls - only provides guidance and code examples
Cannot access your Azure subscription or vault data
Does not modify or deploy resources in your Azure account
Limited to the features available in the Azure Key Vault Rust SDK

use_cases.title: Rust Developer Integrating Azure Key Vault
use_cases.description: A developer building a Rust application that needs to store and retrieve secrets from Azure Key Vault. The skill generates boilerplate code and explains authentication setup.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Managing Secrets
use_cases.description: A DevOps engineer looking to automate secret management in CI/CD pipelines using Rust. The skill provides guidance on secret rotation and secure access patterns.

use_cases.title: Learning Azure SDK for Rust
use_cases.description: A developer new to Azure wanting to understand how the Key Vault SDK works in Rust. The skill provides educational examples and explains core concepts.
use_cases.target_user: Developers learning Azure services with Rust

prompt_templates.title: Basic Secret Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to retrieve a secret from Azure Key Vault using the Rust SDK. Include authentication setup and the code for getting a secret value.

prompt_templates.title: Listing All Secrets
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I list all secrets in an Azure Key Vault using the Rust SDK? Show me the async code pattern for enumerating secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Setting a New Secret
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Rust code to set a new secret in Azure Key Vault. Include how to set secret attributes like expiration date.

prompt_templates.title: Secret Rotation Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the recommended approach for implementing secret rotation with the Azure Key Vault Rust SDK? Include code examples for updating secrets.

output_examples.input: Show me how to retrieve a secret from Azure Key Vault using the Rust SDK
output_examples.output:
To retrieve a secret from Azure Key Vault with the Rust SDK, you'll need to set up authentication and use the SecretClient. Here's a basic example:

1. Add the Azure Key Vault secrets crate to your Cargo.toml
2. Configure your Azure credentials (via environment variable or managed identity)
3. Use the SecretClient to get the secret

The key components are: the SecretClient builder, the get_secret method, and handling the SecretBundle response.

output_examples.input: How do I list all secrets in my Key Vault?
output_examples.output: To list all secrets, you can use the list_secrets operation on the SecretClient. This returns a paged iterator of secrets. You'll need to handle pagination for vaults with many secrets. The response includes secret identifiers, attributes, and tags.

best_practices: Use managed identity or service principal authentication instead of embedding credentials in code
Enable secret versioning in Azure Key Vault to maintain history for rotation
Use the async API (azure_security_keyvault) for better performance in production applications
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode secrets in source code or configuration files - always use Key Vault
Avoid synchronously waiting on async operations - use proper async/await patterns
Do not grant excessive permissions to applications - follow least privilege principle

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Key Vault from my Rust application?
faq.answer: You can authenticate using managed identity (recommended for Azure-hosted apps), service principal with client secret, or Azure CLI credentials. The SDK uses the Azure Identity crate for authentication.

faq.question: Can this skill access my actual Key Vault?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides guidance and code examples. It cannot make actual API calls to Azure or access your vault data.

faq.question: What Rust version do I need?
faq.answer: The Azure Key Vault SDK for Rust requires Rust 1.56 or later. Some features may require newer versions.

faq.question: How do I handle secret expiration?
faq.answer: When setting a secret, you can configure the not_before and expires_on attributes. The SDK provides methods to check if a secret is valid based on these dates.

faq.question: Can I use this with Azure Stack?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure a custom cloud endpoint to connect to Azure Stack Hub or Azure Stack HCI by setting the vault_url to your environment.

faq.question: How do I handle rate limiting?
faq.answer: Azure Key Vault has transaction limits. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff using the azure_core::retry policies. Consider caching secrets when appropriate.
